purpose: Detect duplicate webhook deliveries using provider event identifiers and payload fingerprints so downstream reconciliation logic is only applied once per event.

endpoints.description: Determine whether a webhook event should be treated as new or duplicate.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Classify webhook events as new or duplicate before downstream handling.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use immediately after webhook verification and before any stateful reconciliation or side effects occur.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a reconciliation engine, signature verifier, or event queue transport.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Verify webhook authenticity. 2) Call /webhooks/deduplicate. 3) Skip downstream handlers when is_duplicate is true.

common_calls.description: Check whether a Stripe event has already been processed

failure_modes.condition: Provider does not supply a stable event id and the payload cannot be canonicalized safely
failure_modes.symptom: Deduplication falls back to payload fingerprinting or rejects with an ambiguous identity reason
failure_modes.recovery: Normalize provider event identity fields or add a canonical fingerprint strategy

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Build a stable dedup key from provider plus event id when available, otherwise hash a canonicalized payload shape and expose whether the event was seen before.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Naive payload hashing breaks when providers inject timestamps or unordered objects, so canonicalization needs to be deliberate.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep deduplication separate from reconciliation so handlers can short-circuit before any business state changes.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not process business side effects from inside the deduplicator.

test_cases.name: use provider event id when available
